name: Tuerong
intro: Tuerong is the wine country's back door — a rolling fold of hills east of the Moorooduc flats where some of the Peninsula's most interesting vineyards have quietly put down roots. Yabby Lake sits at the centre of it, a Halliday Top 100 winery with one of the most relaxed cellar-door programs on the Peninsula, but the same roads will take you to Dexter Wines, Dromana Estate's newer site and a clutch of low-volume producers whose fruit ends up in bottles you'll see in the best wine bars in Melbourne. The landscape is softer here — more cattle than vines if you take the wrong turn — which is part of why a morning spent looking for cellar doors in Tuerong feels like travel rather than tourism.

heroImage.alt: Tuerong vineyard rows running down toward a quiet farm road
